title: 10 best herbal tinctures for itchy ears
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for itchy ears are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, often used in ear drops to alleviate itching. They can be a natural and effective alternative to over-the-counter medications. Examples of such herbs include mullein, garlic, and St. John's Wort. Mullein has anti-inflammatory properties, garlic has antibacterial and antifungal properties, and St. John's Wort has soothing properties. These herbal tinctures can provide relief from itchy ears, reduce inflammation, and fight potential infections. By improving ear health, they can enhance overall quality of life, especially for those prone to ear discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with itchy ears because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate irritation and discomfort in the ear canal. The herb's active compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, work to reduce swelling and inflammation, providing relief for itchy ears. Additionally, yarrow tincture has natural antimicrobial and antiseptic qualities that can help prevent and treat ear infections, which are often a cause of itchy ears. Its soothing properties can also help to calm the nerve endings in the ear, reducing the urge to scratch and providing quick and effective relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and irritation in the ear canal, providing relief from itchiness. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of yarrow tincture help fight off any potential ear infections that could be causing itchiness, thanks to the active constituents like azulene, salicylic acid, and tannins.
Astringent: Yarrow tincture's astringent properties can help dry up excess fluid in the ear, reducing the likelihood of moisture-related itchiness, which is attributed to tannins and volatile oils.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of yarrow tincture help alleviate ear pain associated with itchiness, as a result of constituents like salicylic acid and flavonoids.

Circulation-stimulating: Yarrow tincture promotes circulation, aiding in the delivery of oxygen and nutrients to the ear, which can help speed up the healing process and reduce itchiness, due to the presence of lactones and volatile oils.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, yarrow flowers help alleviate itching and inflammation in ears.
Leaves: Containing soothing compounds, yarrow leaves can provide relief from itchiness and irritation in the ears.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds, with their potent essential oils, are excellent for reducing ear itchiness and promoting ear health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried yarrow flowers at peak potency in mid-summer.
Chop the yarrow material into small pieces and combine with vodka at 20%.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 2 weeks, shaking daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with itchy ears because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe inflammation and irritation in the ear canal. The herb contains flavonoids and carotenoids that possess healing and anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. Moreover, pot marigold tincture can help eliminate earwax buildup, which is a common cause of itchy ears. Its antimicrobial properties can also help prevent ear infections that can exacerbate itching. Overall, pot marigold tincture can provide a natural and effective solution for itchy ears, promoting ear health and preventing further complications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce inflammation in the ear canal, which is important for itchy ears as inflammation can exacerbate itching and discomfort. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects.

Antimicrobial: The tincture helps fight bacterial and fungal infections in the ear, which can cause itching and other symptoms. The antimicrobial activity is attributed to the presence of essential oils, flavonoids, and alkaloids.

Analgesic: Pot marigold tincture helps alleviate pain associated with itchy ears. This is important as scratching the ears can cause further irritation and pain. The analgesic effect is primarily due to the presence of alkaloids.
Antipruritic: The tincture helps reduce itching, which is the primary symptom of itchy ears. This property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids, which have antipruritic effects.

Vulnerary: Pot marigold tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration in the ear canal. This is important for itchy ears as scratching can cause minor wounds and irritation. The vulnerary effect is primarily due to the presence of tannins and saponins.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are used in medicinal tinctures for itchy ears due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant have soothing effects, making them a suitable addition to tinctures for itchy ears.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are rich in antioxidants and can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the ears when used in tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula officinalis flowers at peak bloom with scissors, ensuring freshness and potency.
Cleanse the harvested flowers by rinsing them gently in cold running water, removing any debris or dirt.
Dry the cleaned flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or air-drying them for several days.
Combine one part dried calendula officinalis flowers with two parts vodka (at least 80 proof) in a clean glass jar, covering it tightly and steeping for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding solids and transferring the liquid to dark-tinted glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with itchy ears because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can alleviate irritation and inflammation in the ear canal. The herb contains hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to have soothing effects on the skin and mucous membranes. When used as an ear drop, St. John's wort tincture can help reduce inflammation and itching, providing relief from discomfort. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties can help prevent ear infections that can cause itching and other symptoms. Overall, St. John's wort tincture can be a natural and effective remedy for itchy ears, providing a safe and gentle alternative to over-the-counter medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture reduces inflammation in the ear, which is important for itchy ears as inflammation can exacerbate itching and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin and hyperforin.

Antimicrobial: The tincture has the ability to fight off bacteria and viruses, which is crucial for itchy ears as they can often be caused by infections. The main active constituents responsible for this property are hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Analgesic: St. John's wort tincture has pain-relieving properties, which is important for itchy ears as the itching can cause discomfort and pain. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and essential oils.

Antihistamine: The tincture can help reduce histamine production, which is important for itchy ears as histamine can cause itching and inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Anti-pruritic: St. John's wort tincture has the ability to reduce itching, which is the main symptom of itchy ears. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and essential oils.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in active compounds, such as hypericin and hyperforin, that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing itchy ears.
Leaves: Contain similar active compounds as flowers, helping to alleviate irritation and reduce inflammation in the ear canal.
Buds: High in essential oils, which have potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for treating itchy ears caused by infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers using a low-temperature dehydration process for 7 days.
Combine 100g of dried st john's wort flowers with 500ml of high-proof vodka and steep.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days in a cool dark place while shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and transfer to glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with itchy ears because it has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and astringent properties that can soothe and heal the irritated skin inside the ear canal. The herb's mucilage content forms a protective layer over the skin, reducing irritation and itching. Plantain tincture can also help to alleviate any inflammation or infection that may be causing the itchiness. Its natural healing properties make it a safe and effective remedy for treating itchy ears, providing quick relief and promoting ear health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture helps reduce inflammation in the ear canal, providing relief from itchiness caused by inflammation, due to the presence of flavonoids and iridoids.
Antimicrobial: Plantain tincture inhibits bacterial and fungal growth in the ear, preventing infections that can cause itching, thanks to its tannins, flavonoids, and saponins.
Astringent: Plantain tincture tightens and protects ear tissue, reducing irritation and itchiness, due to its tannins.
Analgesic: Plantain tincture alleviates ear pain associated with itchiness, as it contains analgesic compounds like flavonoids and iridoids.
Demulcent: Plantain tincture forms a soothing film over the ear lining, protecting it from irritants and reducing itchiness, due to its mucilage content.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the plantain plant are commonly used to make medicinal tinctures for itchy ears due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Seeds: The seeds of the plantain plant contain mucilage, which can help to reduce inflammation and itching in the ears.
Flowers: The flowers of the plantain plant have antimicrobial properties, making them useful for treating ear infections that may cause itching.
Bark: The bark of the plantain plant contains tannins, which can help to reduce inflammation and provide relief from itchy ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves early in the morning, selecting healthy plants with no signs of pests or disease.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris, then pat dry with a clean towel.
Chop the washed leaves into small pieces and combine them with 100-proof vodka at a ratio of 1:1, ensuring all plant material is fully submerged in liquid.
Store the mixture in a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate maceration.
After the specified preparation time, strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the plant material and labeling the resulting liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Great mullein tincture helps with itchy ears because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can alleviate irritation and reduce inflammation in the ear canal. The herb's mucilage content also helps to soothe and protect the delicate tissues inside the ear, providing relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, great mullein has been traditionally used to treat earaches, ear infections, and other ear-related conditions, making it a popular natural remedy for itchy ears. Its potent healing properties make it an effective and gentle solution for finding relief from persistent itchy ears.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Great mullein tincture helps reduce inflammation in the ear canal, which is important for itchy ears as inflammation can exacerbate itching and discomfort. This property is attributed to the plant's iridoid glycosides and flavonoids.
Antipruritic: Mullein has antipruritic properties, which help alleviate itching, making it an ideal remedy for itchy ears. This is due to the plant's saponins and tannins.

Analgesic: Great mullein tincture has pain-relieving qualities, which are essential for treating itchy ears as itching can cause pain and discomfort. The plant's flavonoids and essential oils contribute to this property.

Antimicrobial: Mullein has antimicrobial properties that help prevent and treat ear infections, which can cause itching and discomfort. This property is attributed to the plant's saponins, flavonoids, and essential oils.

Demulcent: Great mullein tincture has a soothing effect on mucous membranes, reducing irritation and inflammation. This property is crucial for itchy ears as it helps alleviate irritation and discomfort, providing relief from itching. The plant's mucilage and saponins are responsible for this property.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The soft, velvety leaves of great mullein are often used to make tinctures for itchy ears due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate discomfort.

Flowers: The bright yellow flowers of great mullein are rich in beneficial compounds and are used in tinctures for their soothing, anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce itching and irritation in the ears.

Seeds: The small, black seeds of great mullein contain mucilage, which can help lubricate and soothe inflamed tissues in the ears, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tinctures for itchy ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried verbascum thapsus leaves and flowers from mature plants in late summer.
Dry the plant material by spreading it out in a single layer on paper bags for 1-2 weeks.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and place it in a clean glass jar filled with vodka at a ratio of 1:4.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily, to allow for extraction of active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids and transferring the tincture to amber glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tincture helps with itchy ears because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can soothe and relieve irritation in the ear canal. The active compounds in comfrey, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, have been shown to reduce inflammation and provide relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, comfrey tincture can help to moisturize and protect the delicate skin inside the ear, creating a barrier against irritants and allergens that may contribute to itchiness. Its natural healing properties make comfrey tincture a safe and effective remedy for treating itchy ears.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tincture helps reduce inflammation in the ear canal, providing relief from itchiness caused by inflammation due to the presence of allantoin and rosmarinic acid.
Analgesic: Comfrey tincture has pain-relieving properties that can alleviate discomfort associated with itchy ears, thanks to the active constituents such as symphytine and other alkaloids.

Antipruritic: Comfrey tincture can help reduce the urge to scratch, providing itch relief by counteracting histamine release and inhibiting the itch sensation through the presence of tannins and rosmarinic acid.

Antimicrobial: Comfrey tincture can help prevent and treat ear infections that may cause itching by inhibiting the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and other phenolic acids.
Demulcent: Comfrey tincture forms a soothing film over the affected area, protecting it from external irritants and reducing itchiness through mucilage compounds like polysaccharides and glycosides.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Comfrey leaves are often used in making tinctures for itchy ears due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Flowers: Comfrey flowers are rich in allantoin, a substance that promotes healing and can alleviate ear itchiness.
Roots: The roots of comfrey plants contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, helpful in reducing ear itch.
Rhizomes: Comfrey rhizomes are high in mucilage, making them an excellent choice for soothing and protecting irritated ear tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh comfrey leaves and stems at peak growth, typically in the morning after dew has dried.
Wash the harvested comfrey material gently with filtered water to remove dirt and debris, then pat dry.
Chop the cleaned comfrey into smaller pieces to increase surface area for infusion, using a sharp knife or herb cutter.
Combine 1 part chopped comfrey with 5 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, seal tightly, and store in a cool dark place for 4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, discarding the solids and storing the resulting tincture in dark amber glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tincture helps with itchy ears because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can alleviate irritation and reduce inflammation in the ear canal. The herb's active compounds, including arctiin, luteolin, and quercetin, have been shown to have soothing effects on the skin and mucous membranes, providing relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, burdock tincture can help eliminate excess earwax and debris that may be causing irritation, promoting overall ear health and comfort. Its natural healing properties make burdock tincture a safe and effective remedy for itchy ears.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock tincture helps reduce inflammation in the ear canal, which can alleviate itching and discomfort, due to the presence of polyacetylenes and tannins.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help fight bacterial and fungal infections that can cause itchy ears, thanks to the antibacterial and antifungal compounds like arctiin and lignans.

Diuretic: Burdock tincture promotes diuresis, helping the body eliminate excess fluids and toxins, which can contribute to ear irritation and itching, due to the presence of quercetin and other flavonoids.

Analgesic: The analgesic properties of burdock tincture help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with itchy ears, as it contains several pain-relieving compounds, such as essential oils and sesquiterpenes.

Demulcent: Burdock tincture forms a soothing film over the mucous membranes, protecting and relieving irritated skin inside the ear, due to its high mucilage content, which creates a barrier between the skin and external irritants.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots contain anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them useful for soothing itchy ears and preventing infections.
Seeds: Burdock seeds have been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including itchy ears, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Flowers: Burdock flowers contain bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory effects, making them helpful in reducing ear inflammation and itchiness.
Leaves: Burdock leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them useful for treating itchy ears.
Rhyzomes: Burdock rhyzomes have been used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making them effective for treating itchy ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest arctium lappa roots and cut them into small pieces for drying.
Dry the root pieces in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or until brittle.
Grind dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar pestle.
Mix 100 grams of powdered roots with 500 milliliters of vodka 45% alcohol for 6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and store the tincture in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elderberry tincture helps with itchy ears because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can alleviate ear discomfort. The herb's bioactive compounds, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, can reduce inflammation in the ear canal, providing relief from itching. Additionally, elderberry has been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial effects, which may help combat ear infections that can cause itching. By promoting ear health and providing soothing relief, elderberry tincture can be a natural and effective remedy for itchy ears.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Elderberry tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling in the ear canal, which can relieve itching. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and anthocyanins.

Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help fight bacterial and viral infections that can cause itchy ears. The active constituents responsible are anthocyanins, flavonoids, and other phenolic acids.

Immune-boosting: Elderberry tincture supports the immune system, which can help the body combat infections that lead to itchy ears. The immune-boosting effect is primarily due to the presence of anthocyanins, flavonoids, and vitamin C.

Analgesic: Elderberry tincture has pain-relieving properties that can alleviate discomfort associated with itchy ears. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, anthocyanins, and tannins.

Antioxidant: Its antioxidant properties help protect the ear's delicate tissues from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to itching. The antioxidant effect is primarily due to the presence of anthocyanins, flavonoids, and other phenolic acids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, elderberry flowers may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the ear canal.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids, elderberry seeds can provide anti-inflammatory benefits, potentially alleviating itchiness in the ears.
Bark: High in tannins, elderberry bark can have astringent properties, helping to soothe and tighten ear tissue.
Leaves: With natural antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds, elderberry leaves may help relieve ear itchiness and prevent infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra berries in late summer or early fall when ripe.
Dry the berries using a food dehydrator or sun drying method.
Combine 100g of dried elderberries with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks allowing for percolation and extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregon grape tincture helps with itchy ears because it contains natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight off any potential infections causing the itchiness. The herb's active compounds, such as berberine, have been shown to have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, including those in the ear canal. This can help alleviate itching, irritation, and discomfort. Furthermore, the tincture's ability to promote circulation can aid in the removal of excess wax and debris, which can also contribute to itchy ears. By addressing these underlying causes, Oregon grape tincture offers a natural and effective solution for itchy ears.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Oregon grape tincture can reduce inflammation in the ear canal, which is important for itchy ears as inflammation can exacerbate itching and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties come from alkaloids such as berberine and hydrastine.

Antimicrobial: Oregon grape tincture has the ability to fight off bacteria and viruses, which is important for itchy ears as infections can cause or worsen ear itching. The antimicrobial properties are attributed to berberine, which has been shown to have antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal activities.

Anti-pruritic: Oregon grape tincture can help alleviate itching, which is a key symptom of itchy ears. The anti-pruritic properties are due to the presence of alkaloids like berberine and hydrastine, which can help reduce itching and inflammation.

Analgesic: Oregon grape tincture can help relieve pain, which is often associated with itchy ears. The analgesic properties are attributed to the alkaloids, particularly berberine, which can help reduce pain and inflammation.

Immune-boosting: Oregon grape tincture can support the immune system, which is important for managing and preventing itchy ears caused by infections. The immune-boosting properties are due to the presence of alkaloids like berberine, which have been shown to stimulate immune function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain berberine, an alkaloid that has anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for soothing itchy ears.
Rhizomes: Rich in antioxidants and have antimicrobial properties, helping to alleviate irritation in the ears.
Leaves: Have tannins that can help reduce inflammation and itching in the ear canal.
Flowers: Contain antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe itchy ears.
Seeds: Rich in essential fatty acids, which can help to reduce inflammation and provide relief from itchy ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried mahonia aquifolium leaves and stems, ensuring a minimum of 10% berberine content.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces and combine with 40-50% vodka by weight in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure thorough extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Bottle the tincture and store it in a cool, dark place, using within 2 years of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tincture helps with itchy ears because it contains anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that can alleviate the symptoms of inner ear inflammation and allergies, which are common causes of itchy ears. The herb's natural compounds, such as flavonoids and lectins, work together to reduce swelling, irritation, and discomfort in the ear canal, providing quick and effective relief. Additionally, stinging nettle tincture can help regulate the production of histamine, a chemical that the immune system releases in response to allergens, further minimizing the itchiness and discomfort associated with ear irritations.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Stinging nettle tincture helps reduce inflammation in the ear canal, which is important for itchy ears as inflammation can exacerbate itching and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, such as quercetin and rutin, as well as lectins and histamine.

Antihistamine: Nettle tincture helps inhibit histamine release and stabilize mast cells, which is crucial for itchy ears as histamine contributes to inflammation and itching. The primary active constituent responsible for this property is acetylcholine.

Analgesic: Stinging nettle tincture has pain-relieving properties, which is important for itchy ears as it can alleviate the discomfort associated with itching. The active constituents responsible for this property include lectins and possibly other polysaccharides.

Antimicrobial: Nettle tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent or treat bacterial or fungal infections in the ear, which is important for itchy ears as such infections can cause or worsen itching. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins, phenolic acids, and flavonoids.

Immune-modulating: Stinging nettle tincture helps regulate the immune response, which is important for itchy ears as overactive immune responses can contribute to inflammation and itching. The active constituents responsible for this property include polysaccharides, lectins, and possibly other secondary metabolites.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the stinging nettle plant are rich in histamine, which can help reduce inflammation and itching in the ears.
Flowers: The flowers of the stinging nettle plant contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in treating itchy ears.
Seeds: The seeds of the stinging nettle plant are a natural antihistamine, helping to alleviate itching and irritation in the ears.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the stinging nettle plant have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them useful for treating itchy ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves in early morning when dew is still present on the plant.
Rinse the freshly harvested nettle leaves with clean water and pat them dry.
Chop the cleaned nettle leaves into small pieces using scissors or a food processor.
Combine the chopped nettle leaves with 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1:2.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily and straining off the liquid every week.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy ears with herbal tinctures is to use a few drops of diluted tea tree or garlic tincture in the affected ear. Both herbs have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can alleviate itching and help combat any potential infection. Mix 1-2 drops of tincture with 1/2 teaspoon of olive or almond oil to dilute it. Lay down with the affected ear facing up and gently insert the mixture. Let it sit for 5-10 minutes, then drain. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day until symptoms subside. Consult a healthcare professional if symptoms persist.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy ears that are treated with herbal tinctures include ear infections and tinnitus. Ear infections can cause itching, pain, and hearing loss, while tinnitus results in ringing or buzzing in the ears. Herbal tinctures such as St. John's Wort, mullein, and garlic oil are used to alleviate these symptoms. St. John's Wort has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce ear pain and inflammation. Mullein oil, which contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, is used to soothe earaches and reduce inflammation. Garlic oil, with its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, is used to treat ear infections and alleviate itching and pain. However, it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
